question: Design a `Book` class that encapsulates attributes like `title`, `author`, and `isbn` (all strings). Include a constructor to initialize these attributes and a `displayInfo()` method to print all book details.

test_cases.expected:
Details:
 Title: The Great Gatsby
 Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
 ISBN: 978-0743273565

Details:
 Title: Moby Dick
 Author: Herman Melville
 ISBN: 978-0142437247

errors: The constructor takes `std::string` arguments by value, leading to unnecessary copies when constructing `Book` objects. Using `const std::string&` or `std::string&&` with `std::move` would be more efficient.

Member initialization in the constructor body (`this->bookTitle = t;`) causes additional copy assignments. Using an initializer list (`Book(...) : bookTitle(t), ... {}`) is more efficient and idiomatic C++.
The `displayInfo()` method is not marked `const`, even though it does not modify the object's state, which is a common readability and correctness best practice.
Inconsistent formatting and verbose `this->` usage for member variables reduce code readability.
